title: populus nigra var. vistulensis
intro: Populus nigra var. vistulensis, also known as the Black Poplar, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe, particularly in the Vistula River valley. The bark and leaves of the Black Poplar have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the Black Poplar include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
